sections.heading: 1. Why Generics?
sections.content: Generics allow you to write reusable and type-safe code. Without generics, developers might use Any, which requires unsafe casting and can cause ClassCastException at runtime. Generics move these errors to compile-time.

sections.heading: 2. Generic Classes
sections.content: A generic class defines a type parameter using angle brackets <T>. The type is specified when creating an instance.

sections.heading: Generic Class Example
sections.content: The type parameter T allows the Box class to store any type safely.

sections.heading: Real-World Usage
sections.content: Generics are widely used in Android development such as ArrayList<String>, LiveData<User>, and Response<T>.

sections.heading: 3. Generic Functions
sections.content: Functions can also declare their own type parameters. The type parameter is written before the function name.

sections.heading: Generic Function Example
sections.content: The compiler infers the type automatically when calling the function.

sections.heading: 4. Generic Constraints (Upper Bounds)
sections.content: Upper bounds restrict the allowed types for a generic parameter using a colon (:).

sections.heading: Upper Bound Example
sections.content: This function only accepts numeric types.

sections.heading: 5. Variance: out and in
sections.content: Variance defines how generic types relate to inheritance hierarchies.

sections.heading: Covariance (out)
sections.content: Use out when a class only produces values of type T and does not consume them.

sections.heading: Producer Example
sections.content: Producer can safely return T but cannot accept it as a parameter.

sections.heading: Contravariance (in)
sections.content: Use in when a class only consumes values of type T.

sections.heading: Consumer Example
sections.content: Consumer can accept T but cannot return it.

sections.heading: 6. Real-World Android Example: Generic Base Adapter
sections.content: A common Android use case is creating a reusable RecyclerView adapter using generics.

sections.heading: BaseAdapter Example
sections.content: This adapter can work with any data type such as User, Product, or Notification.

sections.heading: Summary
items: Generic classes and functions improve code reusability.
Upper bounds (<T : Type>) restrict allowed types.
